chapter xlii
finis
man cannot prophesy
love be no oracle
fear sometimes imagine a vain thing
those year of absence
how have i sicken over their anticipation
the woe they must bring seem certain as death
i know the nature of their course i never have doubt how it would harrow as it go
the juggernaut on his car tower there a grim load
see he draw nigh bury his broad wheel in the oppressed soil--i the prostrate votary--feel beforehand the annihilate craunch
strange to say--strange yet true and own many parallel in life s experience--that anticipatory craunch prove all--yes--nearly all the torture
the great juggernaut in his great chariot draw on lofty loud and sullen
he pass quietly like a shadow sweep the sky at noon
nothing but a chilling dimness be see or feel
i look up
chariot and demon charioteer be go by the votary still live
m
emanuel be away three year
reader they be the three happy year of my life
do you scout the paradox
listen
i commence my school i work--i work hard
i deem myself the steward of his property and determine god willing to render a good account
pupil come--burgher at first--a high class ere long
about the middle of the second year an unexpected chance throw into my hand an additional hundred pound one day i receive from england a letter contain that sum
it come from mr
marchmont the cousin and heir of my dear and dead mistress
he be just recover from a dangerous illness the money be a peace-offering to his conscience reproach he in the matter of i know not what paper or memoranda find after his kinswoman s death--name or recommend lucy snowe
mrs
barrett have give he my address
how far his conscience have be sin against i never inquire
i ask no question but take the cash and make it useful
with this hundred pound i venture to take the house adjoin mine
i would not leave that which m
paul have choose in which he have leave and where he expect again to find i
my externat become a pensionnat that also prosper
the secret of my success do not lie so much in myself in any endowment any power of mine as in a new state of circumstance a wonderfully change life a relieved heart
the spring which move my energy lie far away beyond sea in an indian isle
at part i have be leave a legacy such a thought for the present such a hope for the future such a motive for a persevering a laborious an enterprising a patient and a brave course--i could not flag
few thing shake i now few thing have importance to vex intimidate or depress i most thing please--mere trifle have a charm
do not think that this genial flame sustain itself or live wholly on a bequeath hope or a parting promise
a generous provider supply bounteous fuel
i be spar all chill all stint i be not suffer to fear penury i be not try with suspense
by every vessel he write he write as he give and as he love in full-handed full-hearted plenitude
he write because he like to write he do not abridge because he care not to abridge
he sit down he take pen and paper because he love lucy and have much to say to she because he be faithful and thoughtful because he be tender and true
there be no sham and no cheat and no hollow unreal in he
apology never drop her slippery oil on his lip--never proffer by his pen her coward feint and paltry nullity he would give neither a stone nor an excuse--neither a scorpion nor a disappointment his letter be real food that nourish living water that refresh
and be i grateful
god know
i believe that scarce a living being so remember so sustain deal with in kind so constant honourable and noble could be otherwise than grateful to the death
adherent to his own religion in he be not the stuff of which be make the facile apostate he freely leave i my pure faith
he do not tease nor tempt
he said remain a protestant
my little english puritan i love protestantism in you
i own its severe charm
there be something in its ritual i cannot receive myself but it be the sole creed for lucy all rome could not put into he bigotry nor the propaganda itself make he a real jesuit
he be bear honest and not false--artless and not cunning--a freeman and not a slave
his tenderness have render he ductile in a priest s hand his affection his devotedness his sincere pious enthusiasm blind his kind eye sometimes make he abandon justice to himself to do the work of craft and serve the end of selfishness but these be fault so rare to find so costly to their owner to indulge we scarce know whether they will not one day be reckon amongst the jewel
and now the three year be past m
emanuel s return be fix
it be autumn he be to be with i ere the mist of november come
my school flourish my house be ready i have make he a little library fill its shelf with the book he leave in my care i have cultivate out of love for he i be naturally no florist the plant he prefer and some of they be yet in bloom
i think i love he when he go away i love he now in another degree he be more my own
the sun pass the equinox the day shorten the leaf grow sere but---he be come
frost appear at night november have send his fog in advance the wind take its autumn moan but--he be come
the sky hang full and dark--a wrack sail from the west the cloud cast themselves into strange form--arch and broad radiation there rise resplendent morning--glorious royal purple as monarch in his state the heaven be one flame so wild be they they rival battle at its thick--so bloody they shame victory in her pride
i know some sign of the sky i have note they ever since childhood

the wind shift to the west
peace peace banshee-- keening at every window
it will rise--it will swell--it shriek out long wander as i may through the house this night i cannot lull the blast
the advance hour make it strong by midnight all sleepless watcher hear and fear a wild south-west storm
that storm roar frenzied for seven day
it do not cease till the atlantic be strew with wreck it do not lull till the deep have gorge their full of sustenance
not till the destroy angel of tempest have achieve his perfect work would he fold the wing whose waft be thunder--the tremor of whose plume be storm
peace be still
oh
